title: Identity, Authentication, Sessions, and Recovery · Decision Evidence Lens: Authentication Assurance
summary: The product conflates account, person, credential, role, session, and tenant while password reset and support override bypass normal protections. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 authentication assurance increment in identity and session assurance model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.
Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Authentication Assurance · Theory Authentication Assurance
steps.instruction:
theory case — Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Authentication Assurance contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Authentication Assurance · Theory Authentication Assurance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence. For Predict Authentication Assurance · Theory Authentication Assurance, keep Authentication Assurance connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. content.title: Commit before feedback

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence. A defensible solution applies Authentication Assurance, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to identity and session assurance model.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
